title: Mocha walnut cake
subtitle: Coffee and walnut sponge with mocha buttercream
excerpt: A classic coffee and walnut sandwich cake with dark chocolate stirred into the buttercream. The sponge is moist and nutty; the frosting is bitter-sweet from cocoa and strong coffee. The combination sits somewhere between a coffee cake and a mocha, without being either one entirely.
sourceNotes: Coffee and walnut cake is a documented British baking tradition appearing in mid-twentieth century recipe books. The mocha variant, which adds cocoa to the coffee buttercream, is a common British home-baking adaptation. This recipe synthesises the established coffee-walnut cake format with the addition of cocoa powder to the frosting.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the unfrosted sponge layers only. Wrap each layer in cling film and freeze flat. Thaw at room temperature for 1 hour before frosting.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The sponge layers can be baked a day ahead and kept wrapped at room temperature. The mocha buttercream can be made a day ahead and kept covered in the fridge. Bring the frosting to room temperature and beat briefly before using.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; two 20 cm tins bake in 22 to 25 minutes

glossaryTerms.definition: Mocha buttercream is a standard butter-and-icing-sugar frosting with two flavour additions: strong dissolved coffee and sifted cocoa powder. The coffee must be dissolved in a very small amount of hot water (no more than 1 tablespoon) and cooled completely before adding to the butter, or the frosting will split. The cocoa is added dry, sifted in to avoid lumps.

content.text: Coffee and walnut is one of the most reliable British layer cakes. The addition of cocoa to the frosting pulls it from sweet coffee into something closer to mocha. The
needs to be made with cool, not cold, butter: too cold and it does not cream; too warm and it turns oily.

items.prepNote: dissolved in 1 tbsp hot water, cooled
dissolved in 1 tbsp hot water, cooled

content.text: Heat the oven to 180 degrees C fan. Grease and base-line two 20 cm round tins. Beat 200 g softened butter with 200 g caster sugar until pale and very fluffy, about 5 minutes in a stand mixer. Beat in 4 eggs one at a time. Stir in the cooled coffee. Fold in 200 g self-raising flour in two additions. Fold in 80 g chopped walnuts. Divide evenly between the tins. Bake for 22 to 25 minutes until the sponges spring back when pressed. Cool in the tins for 5 minutes, then turn out onto a rack to cool completely.
Make the mocha buttercream and assemble

Beat 200 g softened butter until pale. Add 350 g sifted icing sugar in two batches, beating well after each. Add 2 tbsp sifted cocoa powder and the cooled coffee. Beat until smooth and spreadable. Sandwich the two cooled sponges with half the buttercream. Spread the remaining buttercream over the top. Press walnut halves around the edge if using.

items.solution: The butter was too warm, or the coffee was still hot when added. The butter should be softened but not melting. Coffee must be fully cool. If it splits, beat on high speed for 2 to 3 minutes; the emulsion usually comes back.
The sponge was not baked long enough, or the oven door was opened too early. Do not open the oven before 20 minutes. The sponge should spring back cleanly when pressed in the centre.
